publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:42:21 UTC.

ead.archdesc:
=======
RMM08138Tracy Dodgson Punk and New Wave collection8138Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections2B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 19:08:49 UTC.EnglishDescribing Archives: A Content Standard
>>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
  
    
      English
    
    
      Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
    
    Tracy Dodgson Punk and New Wave collection
    
      Dodgson, Tracy Ellen, 1962-2016
    
    8138
    
      1.5 cubic feet.
    
    1970-2007
    The Tracy Dodgson Punk and New Wave Collection contains posters, flyers, zines, magazines, stickers, catalogs, correspondence, interviews, newspaper and magazine clippings, photos, and other ephemera collected by Tracy Dodgson (1962-2006), the founder of Zero Archives (1997), documenting Punk and New Wave Music and Culture from 1975 to 2007.
    15294701
  
  
    
Content Description

The Tracy Dodgson Punk and New Wave Collection documents the Punk and New Wave Music Scene and Culture mainly in the US, dating from 1975 to 2007, through Zero Archives, a non-profit organization founded by Tracy Dodgson (1962-2006) in 1997.  
The collection contains posters, flyers, zines, magazines, stickers, catalogs, correspondence, interviews, newspaper and magazine clippings, and other ephemera. There are also a few photos from various artists, programs, and album cover photocopies.  
  
    
Biographical / Historical

Tracy Dodgson (1962-2006) was born in Canandaigua, NY, and lived most of her life in the Rochester area, NY. In 1997, she founded Zero Archives, a non-profit organization aspiring to focus on collecting, preserving, and disseminating Punk Rock Music History, Lifestyle, and Culture. Dodgson defined Punk Rock music in the broadest sense, including sub-genres like punk, new wave, industrial, Goth, Ska (1977 punk style), and otherwise termed New Music and Alternative Undergrown. She gradually developed this Archive through donations and purchases and actively sought out new and unique bands to document. During the 1980s she hosted a regular country/western music radio program to get hands-on experience in the radio industry. She worked the overnight shift and learned all aspects of program production. It was during this period that she began to buy and sell records, creating her own business that continued for most of her life. Interviewing band members was her favorite activity, along with collecting posters, zines, and other punk movement memorabilia. 
Tracy was the great granddaughter of the engineer Mr. Frank LaMonte Dodgson, who graduated from Cornell University in 1889 and made contributions to the railroad industry.  
  
    
Preferred Citation

Tracy Dodgson Punk and New Wave collection, #8138.  Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.  
  
    Punk rock music.
    Punk rock music -- Posters.
    New wave music.
    Stickers.
    Zines.
    Fliers (printed matter).
  
  15294701

  Series I. Zero Archives Records1979-2007Autographs1979-19991 1
Scope and Contents
Includes Captain Sensible; The Loudmouths with a signed flyer; inscriptions and other publicity material.Biographical Information1997-20071 2
Scope and Contents
Includes Zero Archive's inventory.Catalogs1976-20071 3
Scope and Contents
Cassettes, Discs, CD-Singles, Albums, Postcards. various record companies: Chainsaw records catalog, Alternatives Tentacles records catalog, Daemon Records catalog, and more. List of Bands.Catalogs2006-20071 4
Scope and Contents
Zero Archives Inventories.Correspondence1979-19991 5Interviews1997-20031 6
Scope and Contents
Includes handwritten interview answers by Jay Heycode. Other artists: Jeff Magnum (Dead Boys),  Tucko- California, Antie Christ, Murder, Buzz Clic.  Includes correspondence.Record Companies and Memberships1984-19991 7
Scope and Contents
U.S. of A.N.T.S. membership information. Club Devo Fan Club. Awesome Records, Reachout International Records, Empty Records, and more.Series II. Photographs1976-late 1990sPhotographs of shows and individuals1976 -late 1990s1 8
Scope and Contents
Includes images of Olga De Volga (OFFS, Versus, The LEWD), Patti Smith, Johnny Thunders, Wayne Kramer, David Kramer.Publicity PhotocopiesUndated1 9
Scope and Contents
Includes Wendy O. Williams (Plasmatics Singer) in a 1981 performance and later.Series III. Posters and Flyers1976-2007Flyers1977-20031 10
Scope and Contents
Includes flyers by Golden Voice Records; Photocopied duplicates;  Included bands are PiL, The LEWD, Originals, Psycho, Dead Kennedys, Versus, The Cramps, Libertine, and more. Concerts in San Francisco (CA), Albany (NY).Flyers, All ages shows1980s-1990s1 11
Scope and Contents
Many prints are donated by Glen Tucher/ Aka Tucko CA and others by Olga de Volga. Most of the material is in photocopies. Included bands are Dead Kennedy, VTKTMS, Cathay de Grande, Comateens, Sayyadina, Cyness,  Nip Drivers, The Bongos, VerSus, The Avengers, The Offs, Whisky, Sleepers, Adolescents, The Black Jack, Screamers, Scorgies, Rockats,  the Farms, Cramps Blasters and more.Flyer and poster photocopies1978-199921Fryers from 1980s1980s1 12Mabuhay Gardens (Fab Mab) Posters1978-198122
Scope and Contents
Posters from concerts mainly by VS. (Versus) and Off. Included bands: Crime, Untouchables, Avengers, Mutants, The Nuns, SSI, Jars, Sudden Fun, Benefit, and more. Some posters are in copies. Some posters are donated by Olga de Volga (see Box 1, Folder 8).  See relevant material in Mabuhay Folder, Box 1 Folder 20.Mini Flyerslate 1970s- late 1990s1 13
Scope and Contents
Artists: N.O.T.A., Rest in Pieces, Misfits, Ramones, Freakazoid, VooDooand, and more. Concerts in San Francisco CA, Albany NY, Kansas City KS, Clarkston, MI, and more.Posterslate 1970s- late 1990s23
Scope and Contents
The Punk Rock Movie from England by Don Letts Poster, Crime, Johnny Rotten, and the Sex Pistols, The Slits, posters with Olga de  Volga (see also relevant material Box 1 Folder 8), The Lewd, Circle Jerks, VS, SSI, Comateens, Suicidal Tendencies, The Red Hot Chilli Peppers, Weirdos and more.Series IV. Album Covers, Programs and Stickers1977-1999Album covers19771 14
Scope and Contents
Photocopies.Programs and Stickers1983-199924
Scope and Contents
Police Image Limited Tour in Europe (1983); Tour Programs; Avant-Garde Music/ Art Festival (1983); The Loudmouths European Tour 1999 sticker; Dead Kennedy sticker; Dr. Strange Records stickersSeries V. Clippings from Newspapers and Magazines1975-2007Magazine Clippingslate 1970s- late 1990s25Newspaper Clippings1977-1982, 1997, 200726
Scope and Contents
Articles, newspaper-printed photos, and ads referred to Black Flag, X band, Elvis Costello, Phil Starling, Andy Warhol, Nico, John Cale, Lou Reed, Sex Pistols, Patti Smith, Mastodon, David Bowie, the New York Dolls, and more. Includes newspapers such as Berkeley Independent, Enquirer Newspaper, Fuck Facts!, Hangover from a plastic feast, and Gazette.Series VI. Band or Nightclub Publicity Material1975-1999Catha de Grandearly 1980s-mid 1980s1 15
Scope and Contents
Includes flyers donated by Glen Tucher/ Aka Tucko CA. Most of the material is in photocopies.Circle Jerkslate 1970s- early 1980s1 16

Scope and Contents
Issue 9. Autographed by Plasmatics Singer Wendy o Williams to the Guy from Bad religion.1998: The New Wave Punk Rock Explosion by Caroline Coon19776 Untitledafter 19846 
Scope and Contents
Interviews with S.B.'s RKL, Spattle's, The accused, Atlanta's Neon Christ plus a special punk editorial section, photos, scene reports from Arizona, Kansas City, Boston. Montreal and record reviews. False Confessions meet AC/DC.
